meaning: n.内部的人,会员,知道内情的人,权威人士
examples: An insider said, "Katharine has told friends it is time to end her career."
A realistic look at the crimes reveals that the offender is likely to be an employee of the firm against which the crime has been committed, i.e., an "insider".

The other party would have acted even if the insider had not done so.
Thus, insider trading laws ignore many people who use non-public information in their investment decisions.
